key_insights.insight: Acceptance is the first step to progress, allowing one to work with reality rather than fighting against it.
key_insights.quote: Successful people go, you know, it doesn't mean acceptance roll over. It means, let me start from what's in front of me and stop fighting it and then work with it and then see what I can do with it.

key_insights.insight: There are instances where it seems like people just can't overcome certain traumas, so it's better to manage them rather than try to change them.
key_insights.quote: We have beliefs within us that are just too hard to remove and they may have come from traumatic experiences... let's learn how to put them in a box, stop them from having impact in my life today, and then work forward from that.

key_insights.insight: The "chimp system" is naturally and healthily low in self-esteem, a protective mechanism meant to prevent individuals from putting themselves in vulnerable positions.
key_insights.quote: The chimp system is naturally and healthily low self-esteem... the fear that they're not up to it is inbuilt.

key_insights.insight: To build self-esteem, one needs to first accept the system is healthy and natural.
key_insights.quote: If someone comes in with low self-esteem, the first step to me is accept this is absolutely healthy and natural, but it's unhelpful.

key_insights.insight: Social media can be detrimental to self-esteem as the chimp system will try to be liked by everyone and will give undue attention to those who dislike you.
key_insights.quote: Social media yeah oh that's a disaster area because again what our chimps do is they wantto be loved by everybody oh gosh and and the evidence is quite strong that if I am your friend and I like you, okay, and then you've got another friend who is not keen on you at times,and you'll actually give more attention to the person who's not keen on you than me.

key_insights.insight: Defining your self-image can reprogram your 'computer' and help your 'chimp' to agitate and join forces to tidy your room.
key_insights.quote: If you define your self-image you're actually programming yourcomputer to say this is normal anything else isn't and that will actually help your chimp toagitate which will then join forces and tidy the room

key_insights.insight: Change is most likely to occur when either there's a massive reward or when the pain and suffering of staying the same is greater than the pain of making a change.
key_insights.quote: The one you've highlighted is either it's got a massive reward or there's going to be massive pain and suffering.

key_insights.insight: Commitment, not motivation, is key to success, removing emotion and planning what must be done.
key_insights.quote: If you look at the site neuroscience if you use commitment that means i remove my emotion and i plan on what i have to do and i get on with it so commitment there's a lot of evidence that that makes us succeed

key_insights.insight: Measure what's being done to motivate the chimp system.
key_insights.quote: If you put something on a piece of paper and measure things our chimp gets behind us now so it drives us to do things

key_insights.insight: It's best to have a good relationship with oneself first, because if you respect and love yourself you'll have better relationship and people will be attracted to you more.
key_insights.quote: As a golden rule, it's self-evident again. If you're in a great place, then you become attractive to most people because you're exuding this positivity and energy. And so if you can get yourself where yourespect and love yourself, then you've got a much better chance that people are attracted to you and think that's great.

actionable_advice.advice: Write down who you want to be on a blank piece of paper to define your human system.
actionable_advice.how_to: Take a blank piece of paper and list characteristics like 'calm', 'collected', 'good friend', and 'have integrity' to define the 'real you' without the interference of the chimp system.

actionable_advice.advice: Challenge negative beliefs by examining the long-term consequences.
actionable_advice.how_to: When a negative belief arises, ask yourself what the long-term repercussions are of holding onto it and how that belief impacts your relationships and well-being.

actionable_advice.advice: When you recognize an emotion, take a moment to understand its helpfulness
actionable_advice.how_to: Check in, and take stock of whether your emotion is helpful or unhelpful.

actionable_advice.advice: Exercise your chimp by expressing emotions or feelings.
actionable_advice.how_to: Express, out loud or in writing, what you are experiencing, even if it means acknowledging that someone was out of order, but know when it's enough and not tip to self-pity.

actionable_advice.advice: When facing challenging situations, remember commitment supersedes motivation.
actionable_advice.how_to: Commit to a plan and follow through with the actions to be done, regardless of emotional feelings. This can lead to motivation following commitment.

actionable_advice.advice: When doing something kind, ensure it is recognized to motivate your chimp.
actionable_advice.how_to: If doing a good deed, verbalize it and make sure the other person is made aware to get an accolade from your good deed.

actionable_advice.advice: Accepting unfairness is the initial step, following through with a plan.
actionable_advice.how_to: First, accept and state what's in front of you, then follow through with a plan for resolution.

memorable_stories: Story of Chris Hoy's mental training for the Olympics where he practices shutting off his human and chimp systems to enter 'computer mode' for optimal performance.
Anecdote about Ronnie O'Sullivan walking out of the World Championship due to his 'chimp' telling him to leave, illustrating the battle between the human and chimp systems.
The therapist had to help a lady help others to get into relationships
contrarian_claims: Low self-esteem is natural and healthy because the "chimp system" has a system in which their is a built-in mechanism to prevent individuals from putting themselves in a vulnerable position.
Motivation may be the driving factor to do things and great, however, commitment will help the person get on with what needs to be done
It is healthier for a teenager to be in a peer group than to be isolated.
Telling someone in a relationship, after they took action and tidied, to acknowledge the kindness to their partner.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - The Mindset Doctor: The Secret Man Behind The World's Top Performers: Steve Peters
